content: It’s natural to question if climate change is overhyped, so here’s a simple way to think about why the Earth is warming. Imagine the planet wrapped in a blanket made of certain gases—like carbon dioxide and methane—that trap heat from the sun. These are called greenhouse gases. Without this blanket, Earth would be much colder, but adding more gas thickens it, so more heat stays inside. Since the Industrial Revolution, humans have burned coal, oil, and gas, releasing extra greenhouse gases into the air. This isn’t just theory—scientists measure rising gas levels and a steady increase in global temperatures. It’s like turning up a thermostat slowly; the planet’s heat is rising because we’ve added more “blankets.” This simple cause-and-effect explains why warming is happening, not because of alarmism, but because of how these gases physically trap heat. Understanding this helps us think clearly about what to do next.
